task_completion.description: Open the source image in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GIMP is open and displaying the image 'clone_scene.jpg', which shows gardening supplies and seedlings.

task_completion.description: Select the Clone tool in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Clone tool (stamp icon) is highlighted or selected in the GIMP toolbox.

task_completion.description: Duplicate an element within the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image canvas shows a duplicated element (e.g., a seedling, pot, or tool) in a new location that is visibly separated from the original element.

task_completion.description: Open the Export dialog in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' dialog box is visible on the screen in GIMP.

task_completion.description: Export the modified image with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'cloned_element.jpg' is typed into the Name field of the Export dialog, or the file is visible in the file manager/desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to perform the edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with GIMP's interface, tools, and canvas, rather than terminal commands using ImageMagick or other CLI tools to generate the image.

integrity.description: The cloning was performed manually on the canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: The duplicated element appears within the same image canvas in GIMP, showing evidence of brush strokes or cloned regions, rather than pasting a completely different image.

integrity.description: The agent edited the provided source image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being edited is the original 'clone_scene.jpg' with gardening supplies and seedlings, not a different image downloaded from the web.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source image is 'clone_scene.jpg' (showing gardening supplies and seedlings). The agent must use GIMP's Clone tool to duplicate an element to a new location at least 50 pixels away. The final modified image must be exported as 'cloned_element.jpg'.
